text.title: Event Sourcing Antipatterns
text.summary: Two critical event sourcing violations: mutable events, and oversized catch-all events.

antipattern.description: Assigning new values to event fields after construction (e.g., event.customer_id = new_id).
antipattern.reason: Events stored in the event log become inconsistent with in-memory events. Replay produces different state than the original, making the event log unreliable as the source of truth.

example.code: event = OrderCreated(order_id=order_id) event.customer_id = new_customer_id # Modifying after creation — illegal

antipattern.description: Using a single event type like OrderUpdated that carries the entire order state as a dict.
antipattern.reason: Loses intent (which field actually changed?), makes projections load unnecessary data, and makes schema evolution impossible without breaking all consumers that parse the dict.
